docs.title: Biomedical KG Target Prioritization
docs.text:
This documentation tracks an OA-centric heterogeneous knowledge graph benchmark for target prioritization. The project covers source selection, Ensembl-hub normalization, Neo4j/PyG export, leakage-free link-prediction splits, filtered OGB-style metrics, non-graph baselines, HGT/GraphSAGE/R-GCN models, empirical ablations, and interpretable target rationales.

The benchmark is a research tool for computational hypothesis generation. It is not clinical or therapeutic advice.

docs.title: Caveats
docs.text:
1. Open Targets scores aggregate heterogeneous evidence and should not be treated as direct effect sizes.
  2. Knee OA evidence is incomplete; all-OA genetics can leak non-knee biology.
  3. Source release dates differ, so edges are not temporally synchronized.
  4. STRING confidence is not equivalent to physical interaction certainty.
  5. PPI expansion can over-represent highly studied genes.
  6. GO annotation depth varies by gene and evidence code.
  7. Reactome coverage favors curated pathway biology.
  8. ChEMBL MoA edges can mix direct binding, functional modulation, and target family annotations.
  9. UniProt and Ensembl mappings are versioned and can drift.
  10. Ensembl-gene hub normalization may collapse biologically relevant isoform detail.
  11. Hatzikotoulas 2025 is all-OA, so knee-only claims should prefer EFO_0004616 evidence.
  12. The graph is intentionally focused and should not be interpreted as a global biomedical KG.
  13. Link-prediction performance may reflect graph topology artifacts rather than therapeutic plausibility.
  14. Target prioritization outputs are computational hypotheses requiring independent experimental and clinical validation.

docs.title: Data Sources
docs.text: Source Version Role License Notes Open Targets 25.06 Disease-gene seeds, genetics, MoA, tractability CC0-1.0 Disease anchor EFO_0004616; OA count read live from GraphQL STRING 12.0 High-confidence human PPI expansion CC-BY-4.0 Species 9606; combined score >= 700 Reactome v90 Gene-pathway and optional pathway hierarchy CC-BY-4.0 Ensembl2Reactome preferred ChEMBL 35 Drug-target mechanism edges CC-BY-SA-3.0 Share-alike attribution must be preserved UniProt 2025_03 UniProt-Ensembl crosswalk and protein metadata CC-BY-4.0 Used for ChEMBL target normalization Gene Ontology / GOA 2025-05 Gene-GO annotations CC-BY-4.0 Human GAF bulk DisGeNET Excluded Not ingested Restricted/freemium Open Targets genetic association is the open substitute

docs.title: Required Audit Fields
docs.text: Every source snapshot should record retrieval date, release/version, URL or API endpoint, file checksum, row count, normalized row count, license, and the normalization yield.

docs.title: Node Types
docs.text: Label Business Key Examples Disease EFO ID EFO_0004616 Gene Ensembl gene ID ENSG00000105829 Pathway Reactome stable ID R-HSA-1474244 Drug ChEMBL molecule ID CHEMBL2107357 GOTerm GO ID GO:0001501

docs.title: Edge Types
docs.text:
Triplet Required Source (Disease, associated_with, Gene) Yes Open Targets (Gene, interacts, Gene) Yes STRING (Gene, participates_in, Pathway) Yes Reactome (Drug, targets, Gene) Yes ChEMBL (Gene, annotated_with, GOTerm) Yes GOA (Pathway, parent_of, Pathway) Optional Reactome

All node and edge tables include stable IDs, source attribution, and enough properties to reproduce filtering decisions.

docs.title: Osteoarthritis Biology Ground Truth
docs.text: Disease anchor: knee osteoarthritis is represented by EFO_0004616 (MONDO:0005416, ICD10 M17, MeSH D020370).

docs.title: Known Genes For Sanity Checks
docs.text:
• Anabolic and chondroprotective: GDF5, SOX9, ACAN, COL2A1, FRZB
  • Catabolic extracellular matrix: MMP13, ADAMTS5, ADAMTS4
  • Cytokines and pain: IL1B, IL6, NGF
  • GWAS hits: GDF5, FRZB, COL11A1, COL27A1, ALDH1A2, ASTN2, FTO, GLT8D1, MCF2L, TGFB1, IL11, SMO

docs.title: Pathways
docs.text: Relevant pathway families include TGF-beta/BMP, Wnt/beta-catenin, NF-kappaB, MAPK, IL-1/IL-6 signaling, cartilage extracellular matrix and collagen biology, and senescence/SASP.

docs.title: Genetics References
docs.text:
• Hatzikotoulas et al. Nature 2025, DOI 10.1038/s41586-025-08771-z: all-OA genetics; use knee-OA Open Targets associations where available.
  • Boer et al. Cell 2021, DOI 10.1016/j.cell.2021.07.038: OA risk variants across 11 phenotypes.

docs.title: Clinical Caveat
docs.text: No disease-modifying OA drug is approved. Drugs discussed in case studies, such as lorecivivint, sprifermin, tanezumab, MIV-711, lutikizumab, GLPG1972, and UBX0101, are evidence anchors rather than validated benchmark answers.

docs.title: Status
docs.text: Accepted.

docs.title: Decision
docs.text: Use EFO_0004616 as the disease anchor for knee osteoarthritis.

docs.title: Consequences
docs.text: This keeps the benchmark phenotype-specific and compatible with Open Targets. All-OA genetics may be used for biological context, but primary disease-gene edges should be anchored on knee OA when the source supports it.

docs.title: ADR 0002: Six Open Sources And DisGeNET Exclusion
Status
docs.text: Accepted.

docs.title: Decision
docs.text: Ingest Open Targets, STRING, Reactome, ChEMBL, UniProt, and GOA. Exclude DisGeNET because its current redistribution model is not compatible with the intended benchmark distribution.

docs.title: Consequences
docs.text: Open Targets genetic-association evidence is used as the open substitute for disease-gene genetics. Tests and documentation should make the exclusion explicit.

docs.title: Status
docs.text: Accepted.

docs.title: Decision
docs.text: Normalize source gene and protein identifiers to Ensembl gene IDs (ENSG...) as the central hub.

docs.title: Consequences
docs.text: The graph uses stable business keys across Open Targets, STRING, Reactome, ChEMBL, UniProt, and GOA. The build must write unmapped-ID logs and fail the Phase 1 gate if seed-gene coverage is 90% or lower.

docs.title: Status
docs.text: Accepted.

docs.title: Decision
docs.text: Use OGB-style train/validation/test splits and negative sampling for downstream link prediction.

docs.title: Consequences
docs.text: Phase 1 preserves enough edge provenance to split only valid positive disease-gene links in later phases. The exact split implementation belongs to Phase 3, but the data foundation records the decision now.
